evidence_quotes.quote: I've been playing video games since I was very young, and no matter the game, I always found comfort in knowing that when I bought a game, I could play it whenever and however I wanted.
with some even saying that 'gamers should get comfortable with not owning their games.'

paragraphs.text: I've been playing video games since I was very young, and no matter the game, I always found comfort in knowing that when I bought a game, I could play it whenever and however I wanted. Unfortunately, that sense of security has become a mirage.Many game publishers now neglect consumer rights in pursuit of profit, with some even saying that 'gamers should get comfortable with not owning their games.' I find that deeply alarming. It means no game is truly safe, any title we own could vanish overnight. Sadly, this isn't just a hypothesis. Millions of people, including myself, have already experienced or are on the verge of experiencing the pain of permanently losing games we've purchased. I have bought countless games that have either completely shut down or lack any end-of-life plan, which is incredibly frustrating. We need stronger protections for gamers and our digital rights. No one should have to fear losing the games they've already paid for.
